tldr: Move from TKC's Japan-centric CPA/ERP tax stack to Fonoa's API-first global VAT/GST and e-invoicing platform when expanding beyond Japan.
why_switch: Need multi-country VAT/GST coverage instead of Japan-only e-tax filing
Want a modern REST API and developer-friendly docs over a CPA-driven workflow
Looking for real-time tax determination and invoice validation across jurisdictions
Prefer subscription pricing transparency over fully custom enterprise contracts
Reducing reliance on a JP-specific ERP for an internationally-selling SaaS
what_to_export: Customer master records (legal name, address, tax ID/VAT number)
Product/service catalog with current tax codes and rates
Historical invoices and credit notes (for archival/audit)
Exemption certificates and B2B/B2C classification flags
Filing/return history relevant to JP e-tax submissions
Mappings between TKC accounting codes and chart of accounts
Open AR balances and reconciliation data
export_method: TKC is a Japan-focused CPA/ERP suite without a documented public self-serve export API outside its certified e-tax pipelines. Plan to extract data via your accounting firm's TKC console or scheduled CSV/database exports arranged with TKC support, and consult official TKC documentation or your CPA partner for the exact procedure on your edition. Treat invoice archives as immutable and keep a verifiable copy for JP tax retention rules.
import_method: On the Fonoa side, use the official Fonoa documentation to onboard: register your tenant, configure your tax registrations, and upload customer/product reference data through whatever ingestion mechanism Fonoa exposes for your plan. Wire your billing/checkout system to Fonoa's tax determination and e-invoicing endpoints as documented in their developer portal, and run parallel calculations against historical invoices to validate parity before cutover.

concept_mapping.note: Coverage and certification differ per country — confirm JP support specifics in Fonoa's docs

concept_mapping.target: External ERP/billing + Fonoa as tax layer
concept_mapping.note: Fonoa is a tax API, not a full ERP; you keep accounting elsewhere
Reassign reviewer roles around new automation
Confirm current entry tier and overages on Fonoa pricing page
Build a mapping table; do not assume 1:1
Expect a transformation step in the middle

gotchas: JP e-tax certification and retention requirements may still need a local solution alongside Fonoa — verify in official docs
Country coverage is not universal; check whether every market you need is supported before signing
Tax-code mapping is the most error-prone step — run shadow calculations before cutover
Historical invoice immutability: keep original TKC archives for the full statutory retention window
Operational handoff from CPA-led process to engineering-led automation requires new ownership and runbooks

email.subject: Migrating from TKC to Fonoa — plan and asks
email.body:
Hi team,

We're scoping a move from TKC to Fonoa for tax automation as we expand beyond Japan. TKC stays great for JP CPA workflows, but we need a modern multi-country VAT/GST and e-invoicing API.

Plan:
1. Export customers, products, tax registrations, exemption flags, and historical invoices from TKC (via our CPA/TKC support).
2. Stand up a Fonoa tenant per their official docs, configure registrations, and load reference data.
3. Run shadow tax calculations against historical invoices to confirm parity.
4. Cut over checkout/billing to Fonoa endpoints, keep JP e-tax filing path intact until we confirm Fonoa coverage.

Asks: confirm retention requirements with our CPA, and assign an engineering owner for the integration. Estimated effort 3-8 weeks.

Thanks
